entry.title: Re: Netting in Accounts Receivable and Accounts Payable in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
@rakhmatilla_tuganov wrote:

In this blog post, I would like to cover a small yet useful functionality in the Accounts Payable (AP) module of SAP S/4HANA: Netting of Accounts Receivable (AR) and Accounts Payable (AP). While researching this topic online, I found limited end-to-end documentation, so this blog is aimed at filling that gap with a clear walkthrough.

Scope of the Blog

  • Create a Business Partner and extend it to Supplier (FI) and Customer (FI) roles

  • Create AR and AP invoices

  • Execute netting using the Automatic Payment Program (APP)

1. Create a Business Partner and Extend It to Customer and Supplier Roles

First, create a Business Partner (BP) who will act as both a supplier and a customer. To do this, use the "Maintain Business Partner" app.

  • Select "Organization" as the BP type.

  • In the "Business Partner (Gen.)" role, go to the "Payment Transactions" tab and maintain the Bank Details (Bank Key, Account Number, IBAN, etc.) as shown in the screenshot below:

Next, extend the same BP to the following roles:

  • Customer (FI)

  • Supplier (FI)

Within the Company Code section of each role, ensure the following:

  • In the Payment Transactions tab, specify a Payment Method that will later be used in the APP.

  • Tick the "Clearing with Vendor"/"Clearing with Customer" checkbox. This setting is critical for enabling netting between AR and AP.

Finally, under the Supplier (FI) role, make sure to fill in the Customer Number in the General Data section to establish the link between the customer and supplier accounts:

2. Create AR and AP Invoices

Now, create AR and AP invoices using the customer and supplier accounts created in the previous step. A few important points to remember:

  • Leave the Payment Method field empty in both documents. If it is filled, the APP will treat them as separate payments, defeating the purpose of netting.

  • Ensure that the total payable amount is greater than the receivable, so that the APP will generate a net payment for the difference.

This ensures that the system can offset the AR and AP balances during payment processing.

3. Schedule the Automatic Payment Program (APP)

Use the "Manage Automatic Payments" app to execute the netting process:

  • Create a new parameter run and enter the relevant Customer/Supplier number, Company Code, and Payment Method.

  • Ensure the invoices are due on the payment run date.

  • Run the Payment Proposal and check the results.

If everything is configured correctly, you will see a single payment that offsets both open items and pays only the balance.

Conclusion

This simple configuration allows you to reduce unnecessary cash flows and administrative efforts by settling AR and AP balances for the same business partner via netting. Although small, this functionality can be very useful in real-world business scenarios—especially when dealing with intercompany transactions or recurring vendors who are also customers.

Regards,
Rakhmatilla Tuganov

Hello @rakhmatilla_tuganov,
This blog fills a critical documentation gap by providing a clear step-by-step guide on netting AR and AP in SAP S/4HANA. The walkthrough ensures smooth execution, from setting up a Business Partner to processing invoices efficiently. Well-structured and highly useful! kroger feedback



Best Regards,
James Batson

entry.title: Traslape en tabla de cuotas patronales para Cesantia y Vejez
entry.content:
Hola!

Nos han reportado un caso que me será muy útil conocer su opinión al respecto. Se trata de la tabla 2025 para las cuotas patronales de Cesantía y Vejez.

Considerando el Salario Mínimo General y la UMA actuales, la tabla vigente es la siguiente:

Esto significa que si, por ejemplo, un trabajador percibe el salario mínimo, 278.80 pesos, ese importe se ubica en dos rangos diferentes de la tabla: en el 1er rango que va de 0.01 a 278.80 pesos, y también en el 4to rango que va de 227.41 pesos a 282.85 pesos. 

En el primero, el patrón contribuye con 3.150%; en el cuarto con 4.954%

Las preguntas que tenemos son: ¿cuál de los dos rangos se debe considerar? ¿El primero o el cuarto? En este escenario ¿qué resultado genera el SUA?

Si la respuesta, legalmente correcta, es que se debe aplicar el cuarto rango, entonces los primeros tres rangos de la tabla no tienen aplicación en la práctica.

Agradezco de antemano sus comentarios basados en su experiencia. Estos nos ayudarán a decidir qué debemos hacer de nuestro lado.

entry.title: Beyond SAP Cloud - Episode 26: Terraform scripting
entry.content:
In this tutorial, we guide you through creating a Terraform script for BTP (Business Technology Platform). The script focuses on setting up two directories and five sub-accounts, following specific naming conventions and a landscape setup explained in earlier sessions. Key steps include defining resources for directories and sub-accounts, assigning unique identifiers, and linking sub-accounts to their respective directories. We also explain how to configure environment-specific sub-accounts, including development, acceptance, and production, and provide insights into using Terraform's provider documentation for further customization.

Takeaways

  • The script demonstrates how to create a Terraform configuration for SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).
  • The configuration involves creating two directories and five sub-accounts, in line with the naming conventions discussed in an earlier session.
  • The script starts with the creation of directories in BTP, defining them as resources with unique identifiers.
  • The directories are named according to a specific convention: <sequence nr> - <name> (<dir acronym>)

    • 1 – Administration (ADM)

    • 2 – Applications (APP)

    • 3 – Database (DB)

  • Each sub-account is assigned a unique name following the naming conventions: : <sequence nr DIR><sequence nr SA> - <DIR acronym> - <stage name>

    • SA sequence nr 🡪 0 = SND, 1 = DEV, 2 = ACC, 3 = PRD

    • 10 - ADM – SND

    • 14 – ADM – PRD

    • 21 – APP – DEV

    • 22 – APP – ACC

    • 23 – APP – PRD

  • The region specified for the trial account is US10, but this can be adjusted depending on the actual account region.
  • Beta features can be enabled, though the account is not intended for production usage.
  • The parent ID is used to link each sub-account to the respective directory (either administration or application directory).
  • The script includes the creation of both administration and application sub-accounts with varying environments: development, acceptance, and production.

entry.title: Re: How to cancel not completed project stock order
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

For in process or requested project stock orders, you need to open them, select not completed line items, firstly click Cancel, then click Complete.

For project stock order line items whose delivered quantity is greater than consumed quantity, you can refer to this blog to return project stock back to inventory.

Henry

entry.title: Single Warehouse Order needs to be created for multiple deliveries with single line item.
entry.content:
Requirement is to create Single Warehouse Order for multiple deliveries with single line item. Can this be achieved in Standard SAP EWM? If so, kindly provide the possible configuration and steps to execute with the Basic EWM system configuration.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Single Warehouse Order needs to be created for multip...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

Yes, it is achievable in standard SAP EWM.

 

  1. Please make sure that the activity area(the location of the source stock) of the picking task are the same for all the outbound deliveries items.
  2. Please make sure that there is no more limitation for warehouse order creation rule , below is the relevant Customizing:
  •  "Define Creation Rules for Warehouse Orders "
  •  "Define Limit Values for the Size of a Warehouse Order"
  •  "Define Filters for Warehouse Order Creation Rules"

 

 

Best Regards,

entry.title: Re: CCode XYZX: Fiscal year change 2025 has failed for some fixed assets (Message number AU271) SAP
entry.content: Dear lyl86, The issue in this case was caused by certain assets that had not yet been posted but nonetheless contained a date in the “Depreciation Calculation Start Date” field (ANLB-AFABG). Once we removed the date from this field—leaving it blank—the year-end closing process could be successfully executed. I hope this information proves helpful to you. Kind regards, Emal Rahman

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud: How do I build dynamic “Current-Year vs. Prior-Year” KPIs
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I’m building a dashboard in SAP Analytics Cloud – Analytics Designer where the user enters a year (e.g. 2025) and the app should immediately show:

  • Bruttogewicht Lieferung (current year)
  • Bruttogewicht Lieferung (prior year)
  • Bruttogewicht Lieferung (prior-prior year)
  • an automatic %-deviation between current year and prior year (Numerical Point widget)

It means, that the user can input the current year in the input field, our script calculates the prior and prior-prior year and gives us the results. 

What I already have:
An InputField for the year

  • A Numeric Point (Chart_4) with two feeds
  • ValueAxis → Primary value
  • ValueAxis2 → Comparison value

  •  

  • Working script that filters all charts to the three selected years:
ds.setDimensionFilter("BDATU_JAHR", { values: [year, year-1, year-2] });

Script that removes all measures from the Numeric Point and adds two IDs:

chart.addMeasure("ValueAxis", "BG_2025"); chart.addMeasure("ValueAxis2", "BG_2024");

–> works fine if the Restricted Measures BG_2025, BG_2024, … already exist.

Pain point:
I don’t want to create a new Restricted Measure every single year manually. I had the following idea: 

Have three reusable Restricted Measures (current year, prior, prior-prior). Change their year filter (value in the dimension, used for the restriction) dynamically in script or swap them in/out without maintaining dozens of hard-coded IDs? My question is: Can I simply create 3 restricted measures: current year, prior year and prior-prior year and just change the dimension-value (2025, 2024, 2023 and so on), using scripting? 

My code, that I have created so far looks like that. Here I just works with different restricted measures for each year, that I created manually. I do not want 2025, 2024, 2023, but rather: : current year, prior year and prior-prior year, which 'filters' (years) will be changed dynamically.

/* ───────── 1. Wert aus dem Eingabefeld holen ───────── */
var actualYear = InputField_1.getValue();
var current_date = new Date().getFullYear();

// Zeichenfolge in das Datum konvertieren
var yearNum = ConvertUtils.stringToNumber(actualYear);

if (yearNum >= 1931 && yearNum <= current_date) {
	// Aktuelles, -Vor und -Vorvorjahr berechnen
	VariableAktualYear = yearNum;
	VariablePastYear = yearNum - 1;
	VariablePastPastYear = yearNum - 2;	
	
	var msg_1 = VariableAktualYear.toString();                           
	var msg_2 = VariablePastYear.toString();
	var msg_3 = VariablePastPastYear.toString();
	
	/* Daten quelle des Charts holen und den modifizieren */
	var ds = Chart_1.getDataSource();

	ds.setDimensionFilter("BDATU_JAHR", {values: [msg_1, msg_2, msg_3]});

	
	/* ───────── 2. Measures tauschen ───────── */
	//var measAct  = "BG_" + msg_1;       
	//var measPrev = "BG_" + msg_2;
	//Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Info, measAct);
	//Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Info, measPrev);
 
	// 23735103-3540-4518-3856-333700453218 - Jahr 2025
	// 42543347-2523-4283-3882-314686436141 - jahr 2024 
	// 32937114-7011-4110-3663-482995517414 - Jahr 2023
	// 29229304-1622-4153-3546-241342908683 - Jahr 2022
	
	var idMap = {
	  2025 : "23735103-3540-4518-3856-333700453218",
	  2024 : "42543347-2523-4283-3882-314686436141",
	  2023 : "32937114-7011-4110-3663-482995517414",
	  2022 : "29229304-1622-4153-3546-241342908683"
	};
	
	var measAct = idMap[msg_1];
	var measPrev = idMap[msg_2];
		

	var chart = Chart_4;
	var measures = Chart_4.getMeasures(Feed.ValueAxis);
	
	for (var i = 0; i < measures.length; i++) {
		chart.removeMeasure(measures[i], Feed.ValueAxis);
		Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Info, 'Removed: ' + measures[i]);
	}
	
	Chart_4.addMeasure(measAct, Feed.ValueAxis);
	Chart_4.addMeasure(measPrev, Feed.ValueAxis);

	
	/* ───────── 2. Measures tauschen ───────── */

	
	// Check
	Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Info, 'Aktuelles Jahr: '+ msg_1);
	Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Info, 'Vorjahr: '+ msg_2);
	Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Info, 'Vorvorjahr: '+ msg_3);
	
	Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Success,
        'Jahr ' + actualYear + ' übernommen.');

} else {
	var msg_4 = current_date.toString();
	Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Error, 'Bitte eine vierstellige Jahreszahl (1931–' + msg_4 + ')');

I hope, you could help me with this issue.

entry.title: Exception aggregation behaves differently
entry.content:
Hey Guys, 

I use exception aggregation regulary but  now I met a strange example and I am wondering if someone could explain me how it has happened. 

I have a data mart adso with the data:

0CALMONTH   Material  Plant   GUID     Tool life
202505                1                 10     $sad         200
202505                1                 10     $DAD       200

I wanted to make exception aggregation on tool life . Because for each month I wanted to show the average of value. 

When I set exception aggregation on 0CALMONTH sadly it has calculated 400 for tool life instead / CALMONTH so it did not work as I wanted. What strange for me is that when I set the exception aggregation based on guid it worked as I wanted it only show  values for each month once. 

But I am not sure why it works like that. It behaved totally the opposite that I am used to .
Could you explain Why I need to set the reference on guid  so it calculate the monthly last value for each material ? 
I understand it that because this is the only value that differ on row level but for the concept of exception aggregation it behaves totally the opposite I would expect it to work

However, the effect I want is that when we enter a SAC page, the number of members in a story filter remains unchanged, but the selected values change. It seems to me that this cannot be achieved through scripting. Does anyone have any ideas?

Example:
By default, all options in the story filter (Fiscal Year Period) are selected:

When we enter a SAC page, the script can set it to look like the image below, with only 2009002 and 2010002 selected:

The above is the result I hope to achieve.

However, I found that only the `setDimensionFilterWithHierarchy` method can control changes to the Story Filter, and the result of this change is as shown below. But this result is not what I want because the other members are missing:

So my questions are:
1. Besides `setDimensionFilterWithHierarchy`, are there other methods to set the values of a story filter?
2. Is there any other way to achieve the effect I want?

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud: How do I build dynamic “Current-...
entry.content:
@denys_zhhuta 

Have three reusable Restricted Measures (current year, prior, prior-prior). Change their year filter (value in the dimension, used for the restriction) dynamically in script or swap them in/out without maintaining dozens of hard-coded IDs? My question is: Can I simply create 3 restricted measures: current year, prior year and prior-prior year and just change the dimension-value (2025, 2024, 2023 and so on), using scripting? 

I think the idea is to make the restricted measures dynamic in terms of year selection. Restricted Measure do have the option to be restricted  by Input controls. So you can create the restricted measure using Input control and then set the Input control selection using scripting. The Input controls could be hidden if  required. The below API could be used for Input control setting.

setSelectedMembers of  InputControlDataSource

https://help.sap.com/doc/1639cb9ccaa54b2592224df577abe822/release/en-US/index.html#InputControlDataSource_MsetSelectedMembers

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: Cleaner than a Spacestation Surgeon’s Scrubs – Why Clean Cores Matter So Much
entry.content:
Marco Pierre White, restaurateur and youngest ever chef to earn three Michelin stars, once said: “Cooking is a philosophy; it’s not a recipe.” The same can be said for RISE with SAP. Simply being part of the journey is no guarantee of success on its own – customers will only truly benefit if they start by ensuring they have a clean core right at the heart of their business. Thankfully, SAP Cloud ALM is on hand to help achieve this.

 

Keeping it Lean & Clean

The clean core concept is a modern approach to designing business processes and extensions in a stable, upgrade-safe, and transparent way. Perhaps best described as a mindset and philosophy (there’s that word again), it’s supported by governance and policies that lay the foundation for a flexible, future-ready Cloud ERP.

When we talk about having a clean core, we’re essentially referring to the 5 dimensions or principles of the core shown in the graphic above. Let’s take a closer look at each of them.

 

Keep processes flexible: Customers need to make sure they design and implement their business processes in a flexible manner that allows for adaptation and customization – flexible in a way that accommodates their changing business needs, regulations, or industry requirements.

 

Keep extensions clean: To minimize complexity and any potential conflicts with future updates or upgrades, customers must ensure that any custom extensions or modifications made to their ERP system are implemented in a cloud-compliant manner.

 

Keep data lean: To be able to get rid of as much unnecessary data as possible, customers need to implement sound strategies such as archiving or purging outdated or redundant information in order to promote data integrity, performance, and efficiency.

 

Keep the landscape reliable: Landscapes are often complex, substantial and challenging. Customers need to maintain a reliable and robust integration framework within their ERP system, making sure seamless communication and data exchange takes place between their different systems or modules to help minimize errors and disruptions.

 

Keep operations effective and efficient: Customers must also focus on effective and efficient IT operations, including system monitoring, maintenance, and support, to ensure smooth and reliable performance of their ERP system, maximizing productivity and minimizing downtime in the process.

 

 

SAP Cloud ALM for the Cleanest of Cores

Using SAP Cloud ALM as part of the overall system strategy is a vital piece of the puzzle towards achieving the cleanest of cores. It gives customers an environment in which they can manage their clean core projects along SAP Activate and the 5 dimensions mentioned above, while simultaneously providing them with analytics and traceability dashboards to track adherence to clean core criteria.

At the same time, customers get actual guidance rather than a truckload of options that may only confuse their judgment, giving them a unique opportunity to re-assess their ALM processes. By standardizing and streamlining their ALM processes, businesses can then shed unnecessary customization, leaving sufficient time and scope to leverage innovations instead – not just as a one-off event, rather month after month, year after year.

 

 

The RISE with SAP Methodology Dashboard, a critical and essential tool that helps to address the evolving needs of SAP customers, has moved from SAP for Me and now resides within SAP Cloud ALM itself. Customers now have a comprehensive system overview at their fingertips, as well as the robust SAP Cloud ALM environment enabling them to make daily deliveries and deployments of new functionalities.

When coupled with other services such as the recently launched Transformation Preparation Service for SAP Business Suite (included as an embedded services offering for all eligible and relevant customers within the RISE with SAP journey), time-to-value is improved and accelerated: presales visions are transformed into tailored transformation roadmaps that support and guide customers throughout their transformational journey better than ever before.  

 

Minimize Customization, Maximize Standardization

The main 3 challenges for any customer about to embark on their transformation are how best to deal with custom code, custom processes, and custom integrations. We can help customers navigate these jungles by using SAP Readiness Check, quality gates, and other features and APIs offered by SAP Cloud ALM.

For instance, SAP Readiness Check helps customers analyze and evaluate the impact of their custom code, allowing them to set up follow-up actions and discover best practices for migrating their custom code to clean core-compliant environments, ensuring smooth and efficient system performance.

 

 

Secondly, instead of creating a multitude of custom processes, focusing on standardized content and thoughtful process design can help customers consume tasks from SAP Activate and Best Practice content, ensuring consistency and enhancing efficiency. The RISE with SAP Methodology Dashboard gives clarity on any system deviations from SAP recommendations, ensuring they can address gaps and fully align with best practices, all the while tracking their clean core progress through quality gates and other useful features.

 

Finally, by unlocking the power of standard APIs (including OData and SOAP) and using the APIs and events available on SAP Business Accelerator Hub, customers can learn how to master the art of creating loosely coupled integrations. By leveraging the built-in integration available in SAP Cloud ALM and using expert insights gained through Integration Monitoring, they can keep things running smoothly as they move towards achieving a true clean core.

 

 

Hear more about the clean core, RISE with SAP and other SAP Cloud ALM benefits at the SAP ALM Summit EMEA 2025 in Darmstadt, Germany (Oct 21-23). Sign up today to join us and other top industry experts! Feel free to share this within your community network and, as always, thanks for reading!

entry.title: Re: Fiori Elements + RAP: How to preserve order of batch...
entry.content:
If you want the order to be exactly the same as when scanned, there is probably no way around custom code.
On the one hand, in the frontend with a controller extension and in the backend with an action and then call create with EML.

Something like this (pseudo code): 

// table select event
onSelectionChange: {
const selectedItem = event.getParameter("listitem")
this.selectedItems = this.selectedItems.concat(selectedItem)
}

printButtonPressed: {
executeAction("printAction": {items: this.selectedItems}).then(table.removeselection)
}

entry.title: Re: Develop Powerful Analytical Queries using ABAP Core Data Services (CDS)
entry.content:
Hello Alberto,

the OLAP cache you know from BW-queries on top of BW InfoProvider (ADSO, HCPR) is not available. The OLAP cache is in sync with the data from the InfoProvider. This is possible, since the BW controls the data flow. In CDS this is not possible. Furthermore,  the data the Analytical Engine selects is user dependent (DCL!). This is not foreseen in the concept of the OLAP cache.

Only a local cache is available, which is used during a user session.

At the moment "dynamic Cache" is the only session independent cache. There are plans for a "static cache" which is similar to "dynamic cache" but more integrated with CDS. Also annotations are planned to control the usage of this cache for a single CDS analytical query.

Regards,

Stefan

entry.title: A CAP Plugin for Flexible Number Range Management: cds-numberrange-plugin
entry.content:
I'm excited to share a plugin I've developed for CAP framework: cds-numberrange-plugin. It's now available on both GitHub and the NPM Registry.

Why I Developed This Plugin?

Over the last year I've been exploring and using CAP plugins in various projects. I've really liked the concept how plugins can easily extend CAP functionality while keeping the original application clean and maintainable.

During one of my recent CAP projects, I encountered a common requirement: implementing a number range mechanism for generating sequentially increasing field values, mainly object IDs. I first tried to use SAP HANA's native DB Sequence objects. However, it has some limitations and requires additional development if you need to implement additional logic on top of the generated numbers. You also need to implement your custom logic to be able to test your app locally.

These challenges led me to build a database-agnostic number range management plugin for CAP framework.

Thanks to the existing open source plugins developed by SAP and Community that helped me to build my own. Especially Create your first CAP plugin by @WouterLemaire helped me to understand the basics of the plugin concept on CAP. 

What the Plugin Does?

The cds-numberrange-plugin provides a customizable and database-agnostic number range management feature for your CAP projects. It integrates seamlessly as a CAP plugin and supports:

- Easily add auto-incrementation logic on your database fields.
- Optional formatting features like prefixes, suffixes, padding, etc.
- Determine when the number should be generated - either during draft creation or on draft activation.

The generated numbers can be configured in following different formats:

- Integer: 1,2,3,4,5...
- Integer: 1,3,5,7,9...
- String: 0001, 0002, ...., 0010, 0011...
- String: A000000001Z, A000000002Z, A000000003Z...

I've tested the plugin with SQLite In-Memory, SQLite Persistent and SAP HANA databases and all works fine. I haven't tested on PostgreSQL yet, but I'm sure it will work, since the DB part is implemented within the standard CAP framework.

Get Started

You can install the plugin via NPM:

npm install cds-numberrange-plugin

Sample package.json configuration should be like:

    "cds-numberrange-plugin": {
      "ranges": [
        {
          "name": "<RANGE NAME>",
          "start": 1,
          "increment": 1,
          "createOnDraf": true,
          "additionalProperties": {
            "prefix": "P",
            "suffix": "S",
            "padCount": 10,
            "padValue": "0"
          }
        }
      ]
    }

And explore the full documentation, usage examples, and source code on GitHub:

https://github.com/abdulbasitg/cds-numberrange-plugin

Contribute or Share Feedback

As this is an open-source project, I'm always open to improvements and contributions. If you try it out and have feedback, suggestions, or bug reports - please open an issue or contribute via pull request on GitHub.

I hope this plugin helps the community with number range use cases more efficiently. Looking forward to feedback and improvement ideas!

entry.title: Mastering Product Eligibility in SAP SD: Listing and Exclusion Explained
entry.content:
Introduction

Listing: When creating a sales order, the customer is allowed to order only those materials that are listed for them. All other materials are blocked.

Exclusion: While creating a sales order, the system prevents the customer from ordering certain materials that have been specifically excluded.

Listing & Exclusion Configuration is based on Condition Technique

Path: SPRO--> Sales & Distribution --> Basic Functions--> Listing & Exclusion

1. Create Condition Table

Select Combination of Fields as Customer and material

2. Maintain Access Sequence for Listing & Exclusion

Standard Access Sequence for Listing – A001

Standard Access Sequence for Exclusion – B001

Place the Condition Table in the Access Sequence

3.  Maintain Condition types for Listing & Exclusion

Standard for Condition Type Listing – A001

Standard Access Sequence for Exclusion – B001

4. Maintain Procedures for Listing & Exclusion

A00001 – Standard Procedures for Listing

B00001- Standard Procedures for Exclusion

5. Activate Listing / Exclusion by Sales Document Type

6. Maintain Condition Records Listing/ Exclusion Using VB01

7. Create a Sales Order with Listed Materials Using VA01 T-code

The system allows us to place sales orders with these listed items

8. Create Another Sales Order with Excluded materials

System does not allow Customer to place sales orders with Excluded items

Conclusion:
Material Listing and Exclusion are essential features in SAP SD that help companies control what products customers are permitted or restricted from purchasing—ensuring compliance with contract terms and streamlining sales processes. By leveraging the condition technique, SD consultants can easily implement these controls and improve the accuracy of order processing. Whether you're supporting an existing process or setting this up from scratch, understanding this configuration empowers better customer management and smarter sales operations.

entry.title: Part 3- GST Implementation Steps for Import Procurement Deductible & Non-Deductible Taxes
entry.content:
GST Implementation Steps for Import Procurement Deductible & Non-Deductible Taxes

 The GST Implementing Steps for Import Procurement, lets see how do we configure the same in SAP S4HANA.

Step 1: Creation of Deductible & Non Deductible Tax Condition Types

Condition Type : JIMD IN: Import GST Deduct and assignment to access sequence JGSI

Condition Type : JIDC Imp Cmp cess Deduct and assignment to access sequence JGSI

Condition Type: JIMN IN: Import GST ND and assignment to access sequence JGSI

Condition Type: JINC Imp Cmp cess ND and assignment to access sequence JGSI

 

Step 2: Tax Procedure creation:

Tcode: OBQ3

Step 3: Assignment of Tax condition types to Tax Pricing Procedure

Refer to SAP Note – 2491302 for implenting the subroutine to be added to the import conditions of the Tax pricing procedure. We have created a subroutine 905, you can choose to add your own 3 digit number out of SAP’s reserved numbers.

Step 4: Assignment of Tax Procedure to Company Code:

Tcode: OBBG

Step 5: Creation of Tax Codes:

A tax code would be required for the purchase order creation with 0% tax code.

H5 - Import - IGST 0%. ( You can create with your own choice of alphabet with your preference)

 

A  Taxcode (For deductible taxes) is required for the Tax calculation to be used during the Customs MIRO tax calculation.

 

A Taxcode (For Non-deductible taxes) is required for the Tax calculation to be used during the Customs MIRO tax calculation.

 

Different Tax codes have to be created on the basis of the tax % as required, in the example shown with only 3 tax codes.

Step 6: Account Key to be assigned to GL in OB40

Account Key JIM for JIMD

Step 7:  Classify Condition Types –

Classify the tax condition types in the view - J_1IEXCDEFN through the transaction SM30 for Tax Pricing Procedure.

Classify the PO Pricing condition types in the view - J_1IEXCDEFN through the transaction SM30 for Import PO Pricing Procedure.

Step 8: Condition Record Maintenance –

Tcode FV11 –

Tax codes with Percentages to be maintained under Tax Classification for Condition type JIMD.

Tax codes with Percentages to be maintained under Tax Classification for Condition type JIMN.

Scenario Test for deductible taxes: Procurement of Material with Deductible Taxcode used H3 during MIRO–

Purchase Order with Tax Code H5

Tax Calculation in PO –

Customs MIRO

Inter the PO number and choose Planned delivery costs, enter the invoice date, change the currency to INR as the customs duty is to be paid in INR.

Change the tax code to H3, enter the customs duty amount, quantity and the Assessable value. .

The calculation of the Tax Amount is on the basis of the Customs Duty + Assessable Value

Commissioner of Customs Cr –

Customs Clearing Duty Dr+

IGST Input Tax Dr+

 Goods Receipt –

In Local Currency -

Stock Account Dr+

GR/IR Clearing Account Cr-

Customs Clearing Duty Cr-

The Customs duty charge is not a part of the inventory cost and it is appearing as a separate line item during the GRN. The Tax amount is not included in the Stock account in case of deductible scenario.

Main MIRO –

In Local Currency:

Main Vendor Cr-

GR/IR Clearing Account DR +

Scenario Test for Non deductible taxes: Procurement of Material with Deductible Tax code used H7 during MIRO–

Purchase Order with Tax Code H5

 

  

Customs MIRO –

 

Commissioner of Customs Cr –

Customs Clearing Duty Dr+

No IGST is to be paid at the time of the Customs MIRO booking in the case of Non Deductible Scenario.

Goods Receipt

 

In Local Currency –

 

Stock Account Dr+

GR/IR Clearing Account Cr-

Customs Duty Clearing Cr-

The Tax amount amount is added into the Stock Account in the case of Non Deductible scenario.

Main MIRO –

 

In Local Currency-

 

Main Vendor Cr-

GR/IR Clearing Dr+

 

Regards,

Sagnik.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANAの会計とは？ ~SAP会計領域の特長~
entry.content:
「SAP S/4HANAの会計ってよく聞くけど、一体何なのか？」、このブログをご覧になっている皆様には、そう感じられている方も少なくないかと思います。

SAP S/4HANAとは、SAPが提供するERPシステムです。
ERPであるがゆえ、機能領域が幅広く、具体的なイメージが掴めないという方もいらっしゃるかと思います。

そこで当ブログでは、SAP S/4HANAの会計領域に焦点を絞り、それがどういったものであるのか、また何ができるのかについて、基本的なところをご紹介いたします。
経理部やIT部門でお仕事をされている皆様、またSAPに関わってくださる皆様のお役に立てますと幸いです。

 

目次：
SAP S/4HANAの会計領域の特長

  1. ERPシステムの中の会計機能であること
  2. リアルタイム情報をもとにした分析が可能であること
  3. グループ、グローバルでお使いいただけること

1. ERPシステムの中の会計機能であること

SAP S/4HANAの会計領域の一番の大きな特徴は、ERPシステムの中の会計機能である点です。
会計領域の専門的なパッケージ製品ではなく、SAP S/4HANAという一つのERPシステムの中にある会計機能という位置づけです。
SAP S/4HANAの中には会計機能のほか、購買管理、販売管理、生産管理といった機能が統合されています。そのため、一つの企業の中にある複数の業務部門をまたがった動きを、一つのシステムの中でとらえることができます。

下図はSAP S/4HANAの概要図です。
会計領域の機能のほかにも、複数の業務領域の機能が包含されていることが分かります。


これらの機能は互いに連携し合っています。ここで、製造業を例に考えてみます。
販売部門で受注をします。これを受けて、倉庫にて製品を出庫します。SAP S/4HANAでは、出庫をシステムに登録すると、会計機能にその情報が連携され、「売上原価/製品」の仕訳が自動で登録されます。その後、請求処理を行うと、こちらも会計機能に連携され、「売掛金/売上」の仕訳が自動で登録されます。（売上原価対立法での仕訳処理）


このように、販売部門や倉庫における業務で登録した情報が、そのまま会計仕訳として自動連携されます。
もし双方の部門が別々のシステムを使っていた場合は、それぞれの部門で同じ取引に関する情報を入力することになりますので、企業全体で見ると二重入力をしていることになります。あるいはシステム間のインターフェースを開発して二重入力を避けることも一つの方法ではありますが、その場合はシステム間で不整合が生じていないか、定期的にデータを照合する必要があります。それに対してSAP S/4HANAを使用する場合は、一つのシステムにデータを記録していくため、ある部署で登録された情報は、他の部署で改めて登録する必要はありません。今回の例では、仕訳登録が自動化されますので、経理部が仕訳入力をする必要がなくなっています。
この様子から、SAP S/4HANAは「モノが動けばカネが動く仕組み」とも呼ばれています。

またSAP S/4HANAでは、会計領域としましても下図のように幅広くソリューションを提供しています。
上半分にありますように、財務会計や管理会計といった経理部の方がお使いになる機能のほか、財務部の方にお使いいただける財務資金管理等のトレジャリー系機能、また経営企画部の方にお使いいただける計画や分析の機能を提供しています。
また、左下にありますように、内部監査室の方がお使いになるようなリスクやガバナンス強化のための機能もございます。
さらに右下のように、基本機能と併せてお使いいただくことで業務効率化や自動化を図れるソリューションも提供しています。


SAP S/4HANAでは、会計領域内でもこのような機能間連携のメリットを享受いただけるようになっています。
例として、固定資産管理が挙げられます。
もしSAPではなく、固定資産管理の専門パッケージ製品を使用する場合は、資産情報を専門パッケージで管理し、インターフェースを介して仕訳データを会計システムに連携するという使い方になります。
一方でSAP S/4HANAの場合は、固定資産管理の機能に資産情報を登録すると、それに伴う取引（取得、移動、除売却、償却など）は自動的に会計機能に連携されます。これにより、システム間の整合性が常に担保されるようになっています。

またリース管理、財務資金管理、連結会計の機能につきましても同様のメリットがございます。これらの機能詳細は以下のブログよりご覧いただけます。

■リース管理

  • SAP Contract and Lease Management for SAP S/4HANA～リース会計基準におけるIFRSコンバージェンスへの備え～

■財務資金管理

  • SAP S/4HANAとFinTech融合によるデジタルトレジャリーへの変革　全体概要編
  • SAP S/4HANAとFinTech融合によるデジタルトレジャリーへの変革　第2弾　銀行管理編
  • SAP S/4HANAとFinTech融合によるデジタルトレジャリーへの変革　第3弾　資金繰り管理編
  • SAP S/4HANAとFinTech融合による、デジタルトレジャリーへの変革　第４弾　財務リスク管理編 前編
  • SAP S/4HANAとFinTech融合による、デジタルトレジャリーへの変革　第5弾　財務リスク管理編 後編（為替リスク管理）

■連結会計

  • 連結決算プロセスにイノベーションを！～SAP S/4HANA for Group Reporting 連載企画 第1弾(全5回)～　製品コンセプトと特長編
  • 連結決算プロセスにイノベーションを！～SAP S/4HANA for Group Reporting 連載企画 第2弾(全5回)～　内部取引照合編
  • 連結決算プロセスにイノベーションを！～SAP S/4HANA for Group Reporting 連載企画 第3弾(全5回)～　データ収集編
  • 連結決算プロセスにイノベーションを！～SAP S/4HANA for Group Reporting 連載企画 第4弾(全5回)～　連結処理編
  • 連結決算プロセスにイノベーションを！　～SAP S/4HANA for Group Reporting 連載企画 第5弾(全5回)～　管理連結とレポーティング編

2. リアルタイム情報をもとにした分析が可能であること

SAPの会計領域の特長の二つ目は、リアルタイムな分析が可能である点です。

こちらの動画はSAP S/4HANAの画面イメージです。ポイントは、常に最新データを分析できるという点です。ダッシュボード画面でグラフを確認したり、アプリのアイコンで重要数値を確認したりできるため、ユーザは対処が必要なものにすぐに気が付くことができます。そしてそこから明細一覧などで詳細を確認し、実際の業務処理を実行するところまで、スムーズに進めていくことができる仕組みになっています。従来はこのような分析処理は、分析専用ツールを利用しないと確認できませんでした。しかしSAP S/4HANAでは標準機能として利用できるため、現状把握から次のアクションまでの流れを分断させることなく実現可能です。


また、先ほど特長の一つ目として、各業務領域の機能と連携ができる点をご紹介しました。この、各業務領域で入力した情報が、管理会計領域にて即座に使用可能な分析軸となることもSAPならではの強みです。
下図は、SAP S/4HANA上で保持される会計データを表現した図です。
一般的な会計システムでは、いわゆる財務会計に用いるデータである、日付、勘定科目、金額、通貨等のデータが保持されるかと思います。一方でSAP S/4HANAでは、それらに加えて、売上高であれば、どの取引先に対して、どの部門で、何が、何個売れたのか、といったような情報まで併せて保持します。そうすることにより、管理会計目的のデータ分析の際に分析軸を改めて付与するのではなく、会計伝票を転記すると同時に、それらの属性情報が管理会計の分析軸として利用可能になります。このため、意思決定の元データとして、データ加工の手間と時間を挟むことなく最新データを常に使用でき、意思決定のサイクルを短縮することが可能になります。

 


実際にSAP S/4HANAでは、こちらの動画のようなデータ分析画面を使用することができます。
現時点までに転記されている会計伝票データを対象に、購買管理、販売管理、生産管理等から引き継がれた分析軸をユーザが自由に組み合わせて分析が可能です。これらの豊富な分析軸が、追加のデータ加工の工数なく利用可能であることは、SAP S/4HANAの大きな特長です。


また、データ分析をサポートする生成AI機能も次々に登場しています。
データ分析を初めて担当する方など、レポートの見どころや分析の仕方が分からない場合、生成AIアシスタントに見どころや取るべき次のアクションを相談し、ヒントを得ることができます。
このようにSAP S/4HANAでは、リアルタイムなデータ分析ができることに加えて、その情報を有効に活用するところまでのサポートを目指しています。


 3. グループ、グローバルでお使いいただけること

SAPの会計領域の三つ目の特長は、グローバルでお使いいただける点です。
SAP S/4HANAは、企業グループの複数社でご利用いただけます。

会計機能の観点では、各社の単体データを保持することはもちろん、勘定科目や組織情報といったマスタの持ち方をグループで標準化することで、法人をまたがった管理会計も可能です。標準化にあたっては、業務改革やマスタコードの標準化などが必要になるため、実現までは決して低くはないハードルがあるかと推察しますが、SAP S/4HANAの機能としてはグループ経営管理を下支えできる仕組みとなっています。

また、複数会計基準にも対応可能です。SAP S/4HANAでは、企業ごとに会計基準に応じた複数の元帳を持つことが可能です。
例えば日本本社ではJGAAP用の元帳とIFRS用の元帳を管理し、同時に北米子会社ではUSGAAP用の元帳とIFRS用の元帳を管理することができます。このように、各拠点にて、ローカル会計基準と全社共通の会計基準の両方で会計データを管理することが可能です。

さらに各国、各地域への対応として、複数言語対応やローカル機能の提供を行っております。
SAP S/4HANAにおけるローカル機能の提供状況につきましては、こちらのSAP Help　Portalにてご確認いただけます。

また、法改正についても随時対応しております。SAPでは、各国、各地域にローカル対応の専門要員を配置しており、法改正を認識した場合にはその対応方法をご案内しています。例えば日本では、これまでにも下図のようなローカル対応を実施しています。


また、その他にも法定レポートや電子インボイス対応といった現地要件に対応するためのソリューションを提供しています。
例えば日本では、消費税申告や支払調書などの法定レポートや、Peppolを用いた電子インボイス対応の機能がお使いいただけます。
一般的にこのようなローカル対応は、要件の洗い出しやシステム対応が現地法人で実施されているケースが多いかと思います。一方でSAPのソリューションを使用する場合は、一つのシステムをグローバル共通基盤とし、その中のローカル機能を各現地法人がそれぞれ使っていくことになります。このため本社にとっては、グループのシステム統制が実現しやすくなります。また本社から各地のデータが確認できるようになることで、業務オペレーションの管理がしやすくなるというメリットもあります。

 


まとめ

ここまでSAPの会計領域について、特長を三点ご紹介しました。

  • ERPシステムの中の会計機能であること
  • リアルタイム情報をもとにした分析が可能であること
  • グループ、グローバルでお使いいただけること

SAPの会計領域は、ある業務領域で使用したデータを会計領域のデータとして自動連携することができるため、入力負荷の低減や照合作業の削減が可能です。また、転記された仕訳データは、すぐに多軸分析が可能になります。これにより、常に最新状況をもとにした意思決定ができます。また、この機能は企業グループ全体でご利用いただくことが可能です。そしてグループの標準化の基盤としてお使いいただきながら、各国各地域のローカル要件にも同時に対応していくことが可能です。

 

最後に、当ブログが皆様のご参考になれば幸いです。
ご要望、ご質問等がございましたら、当社営業など問い合わせ窓口までご連絡いただければと存じます。

entry.title: Support for Customizable Load-Balancing Algorithms
entry.content:
TL;DR: We have added support for configuring the load-balancing algorithm of routes in SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and environment. See the links in additional resources for details on how to configure it.

SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry Runtime

Cloud Foundry is an open-source PaaS designed to run Twelve-Factor applications. Those applications can then be called via HTTP by attaching a route to them. A route is basically a domain and a path like service.example.com/api. The routing stack of Cloud Foundry then routes any incoming request to the appropriate application. Most applications will run with multiple instances to increase their availability and scale to accommodate the increasing load from clients. To decide which instance of an application should receive a given request, the routing stack implements load-balancing algorithms.

Load-Balancing

In general, the goal of load-balancing is to distribute load as evenly as possible across the available instances, as imbalances can lead to some instances being overloaded while others are idling, causing disruptions / degradation of the service. There are several strategies to distribute requests:

Round-Robin

Probably the most popular load-balancing algorithm is round-robin. In round-robin, the load balancer iterates over all backends in order, sending one request at a time to each.

Being a very simple algorithm, it has its limits when load patterns are heterogeneous, but it's widely available and is the default algorithm used in SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and environment.

Least-Connection

An alternative to round-robin is least-connection. As the name implies, when deciding where to send a request, the load-balancer first determines how many active connections it has to each backend and sends the request to the backend with the least. This is beneficial if an application supports long-running requests as well as rather short ones. In such cases, with round-robin, there is a chance that long-running requests accumulate in some instances, while others receive only short requests, resulting in an uneven distribution of load. The least-connection method mitigates this by assuming that the number of active connections correlate with load. It then tries to even out load by directing requests to instances with fewer connections.

We have now added the option for developers to configure this algorithm for their application routes.  

Hash-Based

In some cases, it might be desirable to have a certain stickiness of requests to application instances, for example ensuring that all requests from the same client IP are routed to the same instance. A common way to achieve this is to use a hash algorithm, which is an excellent way of turning arbitrary complex input into evenly distributed numbers. By properly specifying the input value used to calculate the hash, you might be able to increase the number of cache hits, if some data has to be loaded per client but not per request.

Currently, such a load-balancing algorithm is not available, but we are considering it as a future addition.

Others

There are many other ways of balancing load between instances, including mechanisms to inform the load-balancer of the instance state via out-of-band communication. Such algorithms require tight integration between the load-balancer and the service.

Configurable Load-Balancing in SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and environment

As mentioned before, by default, every route uses round-robin as load-balancing algorithm. We have now released an extension to the routing-stack which allows users to choose either round-robin or least-connection as the load-balancing algorithm for their route.

Configuring the Load-Balancing Algorithm

Depending on how you deploy your applications, there are several options to configure your route. The most straightforward way for existing routes is to configure them using the cf-CLI (you'll need version v8.10.0 or newer):

cf update-route example.com -n service --path /api -o loadbalancing=least-connection

This will set the load-balancing algorithm for the route service.example.com/api to least-connection.

An alternative approach is to configure the algorithm as part of the app manifest which is a common way of deploying applications to Cloud Foundry:

applications:
- name: example
  routes:
  - route: service.example.com/api
    options:
      loadbalancing: least-connection

Choosing an Algorithm

The first thing to note is that, if your application is working fine, you can just leave it as-is.

If you experience issues with uneven load distribution across your application instances, you could try switching from the default round-robin to least-connection as an alternative. However, this always has the risk of causing unintended side effects. When making such a change, ensure to properly test it before rolling it out to production environments.

The least-connection algorithm may produce better results if you use the Application Autoscaler service. This algorithm will direct the traffic to the new instances that the Application Autoscaler creates when scaling out, because they don't have any active connections. By contrast, with the round-robin algorithm traffic would even out more slowly as connections created before the new instances became available finish.

Additional Resources

  • Cloud Foundry Per-Route Options
  • Cloud Foundry App Manifests
  • Application Autoscaler

entry.title: 🇮🇳 SAP Build: Create Event-Based Processes (Bengaluru, India)
entry.content:
This is the all-new SAP CodeJam for SAP Build low-code/no-code tooling that focuses on giving you hands-on experience for building event-triggered processes with SAP Build Process Automation.

You will build an event-based scenario that lets users create S/4HANA business partners, setting off a process where:

  1. An employee approves the creation of a site access badge.
  2. A second employee, determined based on the details of the business partner, may also need to approve the badge.
  3. The process calls an external, 3rd-party badge system and waits to be called back.
  4. A user creates the badge and the badge system calls back the process instance.
  5. A notification with the badge ID is sent.

The scenario is built in 2 halves:

  • SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh: You will learn about events, CloudEvents, events in the SAP landscape, the process for publishing and subscribing to events, and your way around SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh. Then, you will create a REST Delivery Point in advanced event mesh to forward events to your SAP Build on your SAP BTP trial account.
  • SAP Build Process Automation: You will create a process from scratch to handle the issuance of site access badges. You will learn the basics of SAP Build Process Automation (projects, triggers, conditions, creating instances, monitoring, and the Inbox), and also use some of the more recent or advanced features (event triggers, form events, waiting for a API callback).

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser such as Google Chrome or Firefox installed on your laptop.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have.
  • Complete these prerequisites and the required reading BEFORE you come: 
    • Introduction to the CodeJam
    • Prerequisites
    • Required reading:
      • 1 - Exploring Events and More in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub
      • 2 - Why Event-Driven Architectures?

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Thrusday, July 17, 2025 from 09:30 - 14:30 local time in Bengaluru, India. The language of the event and the content will be English.

Event Address: Infosys Technologies Limited, Plot No. 44, Hosur Main Road, Electronic City Phase 1, Bangalore

  • The event is kindly being hosted by Infosys Technologies Limited – THANK YOU!
  • The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates @ajay_soreng, @ajmaradiaga and @eshrinivasan .

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 25 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. About 2 weeks before the event, you will be asked to confirm your attendance.

entry.title: Unlocking the Power of Metadata Framework (MDF) in SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Unlocking the Power of Metadata Framework (MDF) in SAP SuccessFactors

In my last post, I explained how IAS and IPS help protect your SAP environment. Now let’s explore something just as important, making the system work the way you need it to. That’s where the Metadata Framework (MDF) comes in.

MDF allows you to build custom features and track unique data inside SuccessFactors without needing code or risking system updates. It stays separate from the core system and upgrades seamlessly.

What is Metadata Framework?

Think of MDF as a flexible toolkit inside SuccessFactors. It lets you:

  • Create new data objects tied to employees or business operations
  • Add custom fields to existing sections
  • Trigger workflows and rules based on custom data
  • Integrate these changes into the user interface
  • Keep everything safe through upgrades

What Can You Build with MDF?

Here are some practical use cases with a fresh perspective:

1. Employee Certifications Tracker

  • What: Add fields like Certification Name, Expiry Date, and Score
  • Why: Ensures employees stay current and that compliance is visible
  • How: Create a custom object, add fields, link it to employee profiles, and set alerts before expiry

2. Skill Endorsement Object

  • What: Field for “Endorsed By,” Date, and Skill Level
  • Why: Makes informal recognition formal and documented
  • How: Custom object, permission rules (only managers can endorse), displayed on employee profiles

3. Employee Emergency Contacts

  • What: Build an object to store up to 3 emergency contacts per person
  • Why: Helps HR and managers in urgent situations
  • How: Create an object, define fields, link to an employee, hide on the UI to maintain privacy

4. COVID-19 Vaccination & Testing

  • What: Track vaccination date, vaccine type, next check date
  • Why: Enables workplace safety compliance
  • How: Custom object, notifications for re-testing, permission by location

5. Internal Project Assignments

  • What: Object to link employees with short-term internal projects
  • Why: Keeps visibility on internal talent movement
  • How: Link to employee, project start/end dates, track workload

Step-by-Step Guide: Create an Object

Let’s walk through adding an Emergency Contact object.

  1. Admin Center → Configure Object Definitions → Create Generic Object
    Name it EmergencyContact (API: cust_EmergencyContact)

  2. Add Fields

    • Employee (Link to User)
    • Name (String)
    • Relationship (Picklist)
    • Phone Number (String)
    • Is Primary (Boolean)

 

**Image source: SAP Help Portal – Metadata Framework**

 

  1. Set Permissions
    Only HR and managers can view/edit this object

  2. Add to UI
    Use “Configure People Profile” to add a section labelled “Emergency Contacts”

  3. Test & Publish
    Use Preview mode, check permissions, ensure no errors, then deploy

Why MDF Matters

Without MDF, you would rely on:

  • Spreadsheets that get out of sync
  • Separate systems that don’t talk to SuccessFactors
  • Manual processes are open to errors

MDF brings everything into one secure, auditable system. You:

  • Move quickly when business needs change
  • Reduce IT dependency
  • Keep everything compliant with quarterly updates

Best Practices When Using MDF

  • Use clear names like cust_EmergencyContact
  • Document all objects and fields in a central guide
  • Use versioning so you can roll back if needed
  • Combine MDF with Business Rules and Workflows to trigger approvals or alerts
  • Always test in Preview before moving live

Where to Learn More

SAP offers useful guides and real-world examples:

  • SAP Help Portal → Metadata Framework guide: MetaData Framework Guide
  • SAP Learning Hub courses with hands-on practice: Defining MDF

Final Thoughts

MDF might not be the flashiest feature in SuccessFactors, but it’s the muscle behind powerful, tailored solutions. If IAS and IPS are the doormen, MDF is the interior designer shaping your HR space.

Whether you are managing emergency data, certifications, internal projects, or even health-related data, MDF gives you control and integration while staying secure and upgrade-safe.

If you’ve used MDF in a creative or surprising way, I’d love to hear your stories. Please share your experience, you might inspire someone else.
